annotations.label: Recurrence relation (informal)

anchor.snippet: a recurrence relation is an equation according to which the

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general recurrence-relation definition; only the linear case is formalized as `LinearRecurrence`.

annotations.label: Order of a recurrence relation

anchor.snippet: this number [MATH] is called the order of the relation

annotations.note: The order is captured as a field of `LinearRecurrence`, but only for linear recurrences.

annotations.label: Linear recurrence

anchor.snippet: In linear recurrences , the n th term is equated to a linear function of the

annotations.note: `LinearRecurrence` formalizes linear recurrences (with constant coefficients) over a commutative semiring.

annotations.label: Linear recurrence with constant coefficients

anchor.snippet: This example is a linear recurrence with constant coefficients

annotations.note: `LinearRecurrence` is exactly the structure of a linear recurrence with constant coefficients.

annotations.label: Solving a recurrence relation

anchor.snippet: Solving a recurrence relation means obtaining a closed-form solution

annotations.note: `mkSol` produces the unique solution from initial data inductively, but closed-form solutions are not formalized (see file docstring).

annotations.label: Multidimensional recurrence relation

anchor.snippet: The concept of a recurrence relation can be extended to multidimensional arrays

annotations.note: No multidimensional recurrence relation concept is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First-order recurrence relation

anchor.snippet: A recurrence relation is an equation that expresses each element of a sequence as a function of the preceding ones

annotations.note: Only linear first-order recurrences are covered (as `LinearRecurrence` with order = 1); general first-order recurrences are not defined.

annotations.label: Recurrence relation of order k

anchor.snippet: A recurrence relation of order k has the form

annotations.note: Only the linear order-k case is formalized via `LinearRecurrence`; general (nonlinear) order-k recurrences are not.

annotations.label: Factorial recurrence

anchor.snippet: The factorial is defined by the recurrence relation

annotations.note: `Nat.factorial` is defined by the recurrence and `factorial_succ` states `(n+1)! = (n+1) * n!`.

annotations.label: Logistic map

anchor.snippet: An example of a recurrence relation is the logistic map defined by

annotations.note: The logistic map is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fibonacci recurrence

anchor.snippet: The Fibonacci sequence is defined using the recurrence

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_add_two` states `fib (n+2) = fib n + fib (n+1)`, exactly the Fibonacci recurrence.

annotations.label: Binomial coefficient recurrence

anchor.snippet: A simple example of a multidimensional recurrence relation is given by the binomial coefficients

annotations.note: `Nat.choose_succ_succ` gives Pascal's rule `choose (n+1) (k+1) = choose n k + choose n (k+1)`.

annotations.label: Difference operator

anchor.snippet: The difference operator is an operator that maps sequences to sequences

annotations.note: `fwdDiff h f n = f (n + h) - f n` formalizes the forward difference operator.

annotations.label: First difference

anchor.snippet: Given sequence [MATH] the first difference of a is

annotations.note: The first difference is `fwdDiff 1 a n = a (n+1) - a n`.

annotations.label: Second difference

anchor.snippet: The second difference is

annotations.note: The second difference is obtained by iterating `fwdDiff` twice (`fwdDiff h^[2]`); no dedicated name exists.

annotations.label: k-th difference

anchor.snippet: the k th difference is defined recursively as

annotations.note: The k-th difference appears as `Function.iterate (fwdDiff h) k` (e.g. in `fwdDiff_iter_eq_sum_shift`) but is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Difference equation of order k

anchor.snippet: A difference equation of order k is an equation that involves the k first differences of a sequence or a function

annotations.note: Difference equations are not formalized as a distinct concept in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence of difference equations and recurrence relations

anchor.snippet: The two above relations allow transforming a recurrence relation of order k into a difference equation of order k

annotations.note: The equivalence between order-k difference equations and order-k recurrences is not formalized.

annotations.label: Multi-variable recurrence relations

anchor.snippet: Multi-variable or n-dimensional recurrence relations are about

annotations.note: Multi-variable / n-dimensional recurrences are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solving first-order non-homogeneous linear recurrence with variable coefficients

anchor.section: Solving first-order non-homogeneous recurrence relations with variable coefficients
anchor.snippet: for the general first-order non-homogeneous linear recurrence relation with variable coefficients

annotations.note: No closed-form solution for first-order non-homogeneous linear recurrences with variable coefficients is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous linear recurrences via hypergeometric series

anchor.section: Solving general homogeneous linear recurrence relations
anchor.snippet: Many homogeneous linear recurrence relations may be solved by means of the generalized hypergeometric series

annotations.note: The connection between homogeneous linear recurrences and generalized hypergeometric series is not formalized.

annotations.label: P-recursive sequence

anchor.section: Solving general homogeneous linear recurrence relations
anchor.snippet: Sequences which are the solutions of linear difference equations with polynomial coefficients are called P-recursive

annotations.note: P-recursive (holonomic) sequences are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solving non-homogeneous linear recurrence with constant coefficients

anchor.section: Solving general non-homogeneous linear recurrence relations with constant coefficients
anchor.snippet: for the general non-homogeneous linear recurrence relation with constant coefficients, one can solve it based on variation of parameter

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize variation-of-parameter solutions for non-homogeneous linear recurrences.

annotations.label: Solving first-order rational difference equations

anchor.snippet: A first order rational difference equation has the form

annotations.note: First-order rational difference equations and their solutions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stability of linear higher-order recurrences

anchor.snippet: The recurrence is stable , meaning that the iterates converge asymptotically to a fixed value

annotations.note: Stability criteria for higher-order linear recurrences are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stability of linear first-order matrix recurrences

anchor.section: Stability of linear first-order matrix recurrences
anchor.snippet: converges asymptotically to the steady state vector

annotations.note: No formalization of convergence/stability of linear first-order matrix recurrences to a steady state.

annotations.label: Local stability of nonlinear first-order recurrence

anchor.snippet: This recurrence is locally stable , meaning that it converges to a fixed point

annotations.note: Local stability of nonlinear first-order recurrences (e.g. via derivative at fixed point) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Stability of cycles in nonlinear recurrences

anchor.snippet: A nonlinear recurrence relation could also have a cycle of period

annotations.note: Cycle-stability criteria for nonlinear recurrences are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chaotic recurrence relation

anchor.snippet: In a chaotic recurrence relation, the variable [MATH] stays in a bounded region but never converges to a fixed point

annotations.note: Chaotic recurrences are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's method recurrence

anchor.snippet: when solving the initial value problem

annotations.note: Euler's method is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nicholson–Bailey model

anchor.snippet: the Nicholson–Bailey model for a host- parasite interaction is given by

annotations.note: The Nicholson–Bailey host-parasite model is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binary search recurrence

anchor.snippet: A better algorithm is called binary search

annotations.note: The binary-search recurrence T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1) is not formalized as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: IIR comb filter recurrence

anchor.snippet: the equation for a "feedforward" IIR comb filter of delay

annotations.note: IIR comb filters are not formalized in Mathlib.
